title: Malty Snappy Canadian Pancakes Batter-Flipped
description: A comforting, malty, and snappy twist on traditional Canadian pancakes.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a high-protein flour for the best results.

ingredients.name: rolled oats
ingredients.notes: Old-fashioned oats will also work, but rolled oats will give a slightly better texture.

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: A small amount of sugar will balance out the bitterness of the malt.

ingredients.name: malt powder
ingredients.notes: Use an unsweetened malt powder for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: nonfat milk
ingredients.notes: You can also use a non-dairy milk alternative for a vegan version.

ingredients.name: vegan butter, melted
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use a high-quality vegan butter for the best flavor.

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, oats, sugar, malt powder, and baking powder.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the nonfat milk, melted vegan butter, eggs, and yuzu-cured salt.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Avoid overmixing.
The batter should still be slightly lumpy.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Drop the batter by 1/4 cupfuls onto the skillet or griddle.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the edges start to dry and the surface is covered in bubbles.
Flip the pancakes and cook for another 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Serve the pancakes warm with your favorite toppings, such as maple syrup, fresh fruit, or whipped cream.
Garnish with a sprinkle of yuzu-cured salt, if desired.
Leftover pancakes can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
To reheat, simply toast the pancakes in a toaster or microwave until crispy and hot.
Serve with your favorite accompaniments, such as scrambled eggs, sausage, or bacon.
